candidates.why: Separate same-level operating expense line, but not specifically tied to Materials and services / COGS. No explicit COGS-linked D&A row is shown, so conservatively do not add to COGS to avoid misallocation.
Component of the D&A summary row rather than a standalone COGS-specific D&A line; using it would risk double counting and misallocation.
Component of the D&A summary row, with no indication it belongs specifically to COGS.
Balancing row, not a substantive operating D&A expense to add separately.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Materials and services'. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines but are not labelled as cost of sales / cost of revenues / materials-related D&A. Conservative treatment is to avoid adding any D&A to COGS absent explicit COGS attribution.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating expense line above operating profit, at the same level as Employee benefit expenses and Other Operating Expenses, so it appears outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket and should be added separately. It is also the summary row for the detailed D&A components.
Component of the D&A summary row; do not choose when the summary row exists.
Component of the D&A summary row; do not choose when the summary row exists.
Balancing row, not a substantive expense and should not be added.

variables.notes: XSGA_COMPONENTS previously selected only 'Employee benefit expenses' and 'Other Operating Expenses'. 'Depreciation and amortization' is shown as a separate same-level operating line before operating profit and is not nested under those selected rows. Because both summary and component D&A rows are present, choose only the summary row to avoid double counting.
